memoryArtifacts.headline: Sharon's Notes
memoryArtifacts.description:
NEHGS REGISTER 1934, pp 270-271
Wheelwright, testator of 20 Feb 1610/11 (Will No 55), born probably at St James, South Elmham, co Suffolk, about 1560, was buried at Fessingfield, 26 Feb 1610/11.

zh.South Elmham, Weybread, Suffolk, England: 英國英格蘭素福爾可維布亞德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nathaniel Fiske於1575年出生在英國素福爾可素斯 艾爾哈姆 聖 詹姆斯時，他的父親William Fiske26歲，母親Anne Anstye25歲。他於大約1592年與Abria or alice Hovel在英國英格蘭素福爾可維布亞德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1650年在英國素福爾可維布亞德享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可維布亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Fiske
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from a farm in western Norway, named from Old Norse fiskr ‘fish’ + -e from vin ‘meadow’.

variants.name: Fisk
meanings: English (East Anglia): metonymic occupational name for a fisherman or fish seller, or a nickname for someone supposedly resembling a fish in some way, from Old Norse fiskr ‘fish’ (cognate with Old English fisc). See Fish 1.

nameMeanings.name: Nathaniel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of a New Testament name, which is derived from the Greek form of a Hebrew name meaning ‘God has given’ ( compare Nathan , which is sometimes taken as a short form of this name). It was borne by one of the less prominent of Christ's apostles (John 1:45; 21:2), who in fact is probably identical with Bartholomew . The spelling used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament is Nathanael , but this is in much less frequent use as a given name in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Nathaniel Fiske was born in 1575, in South Elmham St James, Suffolk, England, his father, William Fiske, was 26 and his mother, Anne Anstye, was 25. He married Abria or alice Hovel about 1592, in Weybread, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died in 1650, in Weybread, Suffolk, England, at the age of 75, and was buried in Weybread, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nathaniel Fiske est né en 1575, à South Elmham St James, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Fiske, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Anne Anstye, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Abria or alice Hovel environ 1592, à Weybread, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1650, à Weybread, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Weybread, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Nathaniel Fiske(이)가 1575년 영국 수폴크 소우스 엘함 스트 자메스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Fiske(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Anstye(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1592년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 w이브리드에서 Abria or alice Hovel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1650년 영국 수폴크 w이브리드에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 w이브리드에 묻혔다.

ru.St. James, South Elmham, Suffolk, Englan: ст джемес'с чурч, ст джемесь соут элмхам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nathaniel Fiske родился в 1575 г. в местности соут элмхам ст джемесь, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Fiske было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Anstye, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Abria or alice Hovel около 1592 г. в местности вибрид, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1650 г. в местности вибрид, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности вибрид, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.St. James, South Elmham, Suffolk, Englan: St James's Church, St James South Elmham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nathaniel Fiske nació en 1575, en South Elmham St James, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Fiske, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Anne Anstye, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Abria or alice Hovel aproximadamente 1592, en Weybread, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió en 1650, en Weybread, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Weybread, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
